per_outcome.operationalization: Self-reported minutes actively engaged in completing memo writing assignments (research and writing combined)
Total points earned on standardized rubric (0-24 points), summing eight criteria each scored 1-3
Single rubric criterion scored 1 (insufficient), 2 (sufficient), or 3 (proficient)
Combined score on two rubric criteria (quality of research and integration/analysis), each scored 1-3
Single rubric criterion scored 1 (insufficient), 2 (sufficient), or 3 (proficient)

Self-evaluation of professional writing competency relative to CMU graduate students, scale 1-5. This is a self-perception measure, not an objective quality assessment of task output.
Self-reported usefulness of AI tool for summarization sub-task, scale 1-4. This measures perceived utility, not speed or quality of output.
Self-reported usefulness of AI tool for writing sub-task, scale 1-4. This measures perceived utility, not speed or quality of output.
Self-reported usefulness of AI tool for web research sub-task, scale 1-4. This measures perceived utility, not speed or quality of output.
Self-reported usefulness of AI tool for policy development sub-task, scale 1-4. This measures perceived utility, not speed or quality of output.
Self-reported usefulness of AI tool for analysis sub-task, scale 1-4. This measures perceived utility, not speed or quality of output.
Self-reported belief about speed improvement from first to third assignment, scale 1-5. This is perceived change, not measured speed.
Self-reported belief about quality improvement from first to third assignment, scale 1-5. This is perceived change, not measured quality.
Self-reported attribution of improvement to AI versus experience, scale 1-10. This measures causal attribution beliefs, not speed or quality.
Self-reported valuation of quality versus speed benefits, scale 1-10. This measures preferences, not speed or quality of output.
